groups.name: pulse
groups.description: Pulse — production-correctness layer for Spring Boot. All subsystems are opt-out via these properties; defaults are tuned for production.

groups.name: pulse.context
groups.description: Maps inbound HTTP headers to MDC keys so every log line and span carries request/correlation/user/tenant identity. Customize header names per your edge convention.

groups.name: pulse.trace-guard
groups.description: Detects inbound requests that arrive without W3C traceparent or B3 headers and emits the pulse.trace.missing counter. Set fail-on-missing=true at the edge of your network to bounce uninstrumented callers with HTTP 400.

groups.name: pulse.sampling
groups.description: OpenTelemetry trace sampler. Default ParentBased(TraceIdRatioBased) keeps parent decisions and applies a head-based ratio for root spans. prefer-sampling-on-error layers a best-effort error sampler so spans flagged ERROR are always kept.

groups.name: pulse.async
groups.description: Propagates MDC + OTel context across @Async, @Scheduled, and TaskExecutor hops. Pulse exposes a TaskDecorator bean by default so Spring Boot's auto-configured executor inherits propagation without override; flip dedicated-executor=true if you want an isolated pulseDedicatedExecutor.

groups.name: pulse.kafka
groups.description: Wires Pulse's producer + consumer interceptors into Spring Kafka so MDC and timeout-budget travel with every record. Auto-activated when spring-kafka is on the classpath.

groups.name: pulse.exception-handler
groups.description: Global @RestControllerAdvice that returns RFC 7807 ProblemDetail responses with traceId, requestId, and a stable errorFingerprint. The fingerprint also lands on the active span and an unhandled-error counter.

groups.name: pulse.cardinality
groups.description: Caps distinct tag values per meter to prevent runaway tag explosions. Excess values bucket to overflow-value; pulse.cardinality.overflow counter records every clamp so dashboards and alerts can spot the offender.

groups.name: pulse.timeout-budget
groups.description: End-to-end deadline propagation. Inbound header is clamped to maximum-budget; the remaining budget at egress is propagated as outbound-header, minus safety-margin to give downstream a chance to respond before the parent times out.

groups.name: pulse.wide-events
groups.description: SpanEvents API — one call writes a span event, a structured log line, and (optionally) a bounded counter. The counter is tagged only by event name to keep cardinality predictable while the span and log carry the rich attributes.

groups.name: pulse.logging
groups.description: Pulse logging integration. The bundled JSON layout always emits traceId, spanId, app.version, and build.commit; pii-masking-enabled toggles automatic redaction of emails, SSNs, credit cards, Bearer tokens, and JSON secret fields.

groups.name: pulse.banner
groups.description: Startup banner that prints the active Pulse subsystems, their effective settings, and the actuator endpoint location. Set to false if you ship logs to a system that doesn't tolerate multi-line entries.

groups.name: pulse.histograms
groups.description: Enables percentile histograms on Spring Boot's standard meters with SLO-aligned bucket boundaries. The default buckets cover the typical web request range; tune slo-buckets to your service's latency profile.

groups.name: pulse.slo
groups.description: SLO-as-code. Declare objectives once and Pulse renders a Kubernetes PrometheusRule (recording rules + multi-window burn-rate alerts) at /actuator/pulse/slo, ready to kubectl apply -f -.

groups.name: pulse.health
groups.description: Pulse-supplied Spring Boot HealthIndicators. The OTel exporter indicator reports a degraded state when no span has been successfully exported within otel-exporter-stale-after — useful for catching collector outages that would otherwise be silent.

groups.name: pulse.shutdown
groups.description: JVM shutdown lifecycle. otel-flush-enabled blocks shutdown for up to otel-flush-timeout so the BatchSpanProcessor can drain queued spans — without this the last few hundred spans before a rolling deploy are lost.

properties.name: pulse.sampling.probability
properties.description: Head-based sampling ratio for root spans (parent decisions are always honoured). 1.0 = sample all (default), 0.1 = 10%, 0.0 = sample none. Set to 0.05–0.1 in high-traffic production services.

properties.name: pulse.cardinality.max-tag-values-per-meter
properties.description: Maximum distinct values allowed per (meter, tag-key). Beyond this the value is replaced with overflow-value and the meter continues to record samples. Conservative default of 1000 fits most services; raise only when you've identified a high-cardinality dimension that's actually meaningful.

properties.name: pulse.cardinality.overflow-value
properties.description: String substituted in place of values that exceed max-tag-values-per-meter. Defaults to OVERFLOW so it stands out in dashboards.

properties.name: pulse.cardinality.meter-prefixes-to-protect
properties.description: Optional explicit allow-list of meter name prefixes to apply the firewall to. Empty = protect every meter (recommended).

properties.name: pulse.cardinality.exempt-meter-prefixes
properties.description: Meter name prefixes to exempt from the firewall. Pulse's own pulse.* meters are always exempt regardless of this list.

properties.name: pulse.timeout-budget.default-budget
properties.description: Budget assigned to inbound requests that do not present a timeout-budget header. Acts as a safety net so missing headers don't translate to unbounded execution.

properties.name: pulse.timeout-budget.maximum-budget
properties.description: Hard ceiling on the inbound budget header. Edge requests proposing larger budgets are clamped, preventing an external caller from setting a 5-minute timeout on a service that should never run that long.

properties.name: pulse.timeout-budget.safety-margin
properties.description: Subtracted from the propagated budget on every outbound hop so the downstream service has time to respond before the parent's clock expires. 50ms is a sensible default for in-region calls; raise for cross-region paths.

properties.name: pulse.timeout-budget.minimum-budget
properties.description: Floor for the propagated budget. If the remaining budget falls below this value, Pulse increments pulse.timeout_budget.exhausted{transport=…} and propagates the floor anyway so the downstream call still gets a sane deadline.

properties.name: pulse.histograms.slo-buckets
properties.description: Histogram bucket boundaries used for percentile + SLO calculations. Defaults align with typical web-request latency targets; tune to span your SLO threshold and one bucket above it.

properties.name: pulse.health.otel-exporter-stale-after
properties.description: After this duration without a successful span export, the OTel exporter HealthIndicator reports DOWN. Tune to your service's traffic pattern — a 5m default catches collector outages while tolerating brief idle periods.

values.description: Default sentinel — stands out in Grafana label pickers.
Fraction of non-5xx responses on http_server_requests_seconds_count. Pair with target=0.999.
Fraction of requests under threshold on http_server_requests_seconds_bucket. Pair with target=0.95 and threshold=500ms.
Pulse default (RFC 6648 — no X- prefix). Numeric milliseconds.
Common edge convention — interoperates with Envoy's x-envoy-upstream-rq-timeout-ms.
gRPC convention — requires a custom parser.
